title: Adding an employee
text: To add an employee, go to Employees → Manage Employees and click Add. Fill in the required fields (name, bank account, and pay grade are required). A new employee record must be APPROVED before the employee can be processed for payroll, accrue a leave balance, or join a performance cycle. You can also bulk-import employees with the Excel/CSV template on the same page.

title: Employee lifecycle (suspend, terminate, resign, reinstate)
text: Employee lifecycle actions are taken from the employee's profile: suspend, terminate, resign, or reinstate. A terminated or resigned employee is removed from active headcount and excluded from payroll, but their records are retained. Reinstating restores an employee to ACTIVE status.

title: Self-onboarding (public intake)
text: Self-onboarding lets candidates fill in their own details before they become employees. HR builds the intake form fields, shares a public link or QR code, reviews submissions, and converts a submission into a full employee record. Find it under Employees → Self-Onboarding.

title: Applying for leave (requesting time off)
text: Employees request leave from the sidebar under Leave → Personal Leave. On the Personal Leave page click the Add button, choose the leave type and the start and end dates, add any details, then Submit. The request goes to your supervisor's approval queue (status Pending Approval) and may then need HR approval (Pending HR Approval). Note: 'Manage Leave' is the admin setup area (leave types, periods, holidays, work week) and 'Leave Calendar' shows who is on leave — neither is where you submit your own request; use Personal Leave. Leave is only counted on working days configured in the Work Week; non-working days and holidays are skipped.

title: Approving leave
text: Approvers review leave from their approval queue. Approve or reject actions are available in the request's slide-over detail panel, not in the table rows. 'Pending Approval' is the supervisor queue; 'Pending HR Approval' is the HR queue. Approving deducts the days from the employee's balance.

title: Leave setup (types, periods, holidays, work week)
text: Leave configuration lives under Leave Setup: define leave types and their annual entitlement, leave periods, public holidays, the work week (which days are working vs non-working), leave groups, and approval rules. Carry-forward of unused leave is handled when a new leave period is activated.

title: Submitting a medical claim
text: Employees submit healthcare expense claims under Medical → Personal Medical. Use 'My Medicals' for your own costs or 'Dependent Medical Request' for a registered dependent. Enter the date, description, and amount, attach the receipt, save as draft, then submit for approval. Register dependents under Relations → Dependents first.

title: Reviewing and approving medical claims
text: With medical permissions, go to Medical → Manage Medical. The Staff Medical and Dependent Medical tabs list submitted claims; open a claim to see the amount, date, and receipt, then Approve or Reject with a reason. Approved claims reduce the employee's remaining annual medical balance, which is tracked against per-pay-grade limits set in Medical Limits Setup.

title: Starting a new medical year (resetting utilization)
text: At year-end, HR can reset every employee's medical utilization back to zero so the new year starts fresh. Go to Medical → Manage Medical → Staff Medical Enquiry and click 'Start New Medical Year', confirm the closing-year label, and reset. This is non-destructive: all past claims, costs, and GL postings are kept; only the counting start point moves, and per-grade limits are unchanged. A closing snapshot of everyone's final utilization is saved and viewable from the History button. It requires the 'Reset Medical Utilization' permission.

title: Running payroll
text: Payroll is processed under Payroll. Create a payroll run for a pay period, generate the data, review it, and submit it for approval. Approved runs can post to the general ledger. Salary components, pay grades, notches, and component assignments are configured in the Salary setup area.

title: Assigning salary components (paygrades, notches, exceptions)
text: Salary components are NOT assigned to employees one by one. Instead, in Salary > Component Assignment, you assign a component (with an amount) to a paygrade or to a notch, and every employee on that paygrade or notch automatically inherits it. An employee gets the union of their paygrade's components and their notch's components; if the same component is set on both, the notch's amount wins (a notch overrides its paygrade). Per-employee deviations are handled in the Salary > Exceptions tab, where you can override the amount for one employee, exclude an inherited component (the employee no longer gets it), or add an extra component their grade or notch does not include. Exceptions always override the inherited paygrade/notch values.

title: Basic salary and the grade-linked component
text: An employee's basic pay comes from their NOTCH AMOUNT, set in Salary > Pay Grades & Notches (each notch has an amount). One salary component is flagged 'Grade-Linked' (typically 'Basic Salary') in Salary > Components; the payroll engine automatically feeds the notch amount into that component for each employee. You do not assign the Basic Salary component in Component Assignment; doing so only overrides the notch amount. To change basic pay for everyone on a notch, edit the notch amount; to change it for one employee, use an Exception on the grade-linked component.

title: Record-only mode (turning off GL postings and payments)
text: Under Settings > Controls > Postings you can switch off GL posting/payments per module - Leave, Medical, and Payroll. When a switch is off, that module is used for record-keeping only: requests and approvals still work, but nothing is posted to the general ledger or paid out. Payroll runs still finalize; medical/leave still record; they just skip the GL step. The switches default to on.

title: Payslips
text: Payslips are generated from a payroll run using a payslip template. Employees can view their own payslips. The payslip template designer controls which columns and amounts appear.

title: Time and attendance
text: Attendance covers clock in/out, biometric device sync, kiosk punching, timesheets, and absence tracking. Admins review attendance under Manage Attendance; employees see their own under My Attendance. Day statuses include Present, Late, Half Day, Incomplete, and On Leave.

title: Performance management
text: Performance management runs appraisal cycles with self-assessment, supervisor review, and HR review stages, scored against goals and competencies. Admins manage cycles under Manage Performance; employees complete their own reviews under Personal Performance.

title: Training and development
text: Training covers a course catalog, nominations, sessions, and certificates. Admins manage active courses under Training; employees see and request their own training under Personal Training.

title: Document centre
text: The Document Centre stores company documents (policies, shared files) and employee documents (per-person files with optional expiry). Admins manage all documents; employees see their own under Personal Documents.

title: How access works (screens and permissions)
text: Access is screen- and permission-based, not role-based. Modules and pages assigned to an employee are the access gate. Users without management permissions only see personal pages (their own leave, medical, attendance, documents, performance). Users with view/manage permissions see the management screens for those modules.

title: Notifications
text: The in-app bell shows real-time notifications for events relevant to you — approvals you need to action and updates to your own requests. Email notifications per module can be toggled under Settings → Notification Settings; the in-app bell always shows.

title: Staff Organogram (org chart)
text: The Staff Organogram is a visual chart of the company's reporting structure showing each employee and who they report to. Open it from the sidebar under 'Staff Organogram' (a top-level menu item available to all users — it is not under System Administration). The chart is built automatically from each employee's Supervisor: an employee appears beneath their supervisor, and employees with no supervisor assigned appear at the top level.

title: Reporting line / supervisor
text: An employee's reporting line — who they report to — is set by the Supervisor field on their employee record. To set or change it, go to Employees → Manage Employees, open the employee, set the Supervisor field, and save. The supervisor link drives the Staff Organogram and routes that employee's leave requests to their supervisor for approval. Leaving the Supervisor empty means the employee has no manager.

title: Leave approval when there is no supervisor
text: When supervisor approval is enabled, a submitted leave request first goes to the employee's supervisor (status Pending Approval) and then to HR (Pending HR Approval). If the employee has no supervisor assigned, the supervisor step is skipped automatically and the request goes straight to HR for approval (Pending HR Approval) — it is never left in a queue with no approver. When supervisor approval is disabled, all requests go directly to HR.
